name: Marine oil shifted lipid mediators and leukocyte phenotype in PAD
summary: A short-course enriched marine oil study in healthy volunteers and PAD participants increased SPM-related lipid mediators and shifted leukocyte and macrophage phenotypes toward less inflammatory profiles.
statement: Short-course oral enriched marine oil supplementation increased plasma lipid mediator families, total SPMs, and the SPM:prostaglandin ratio, increased peripheral monocyte and neutrophil phagocytic activity, reduced multiple pro-inflammatory monocyte markers, and shifted monocyte-derived macrophage expression toward a reparative phenotype, with the largest changes in PAD participants.
population_or_model: Healthy volunteers and participants with symptomatic peripheral artery disease
quantitative_note: The source reports significant increases in plasma SPM measures and dose-dependent SPM:prostaglandin ratio changes, with no clinical outcome claim.
caveats: The study was small and open-label.
Peripheral artery disease biomarker changes do not establish aging or tissue-repair efficacy.
The registry has no posted results in the current June 2, 2026 view.
